name: Saute of Shrimp with Bergamot Infusion
recipeIngredient: 84 Raw headless Mexican shrimp, peeled and deveined
4 Red bell peppers, roasted and peeled
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the papaya salsa first to allow flavors to combine. Place papaya, lime zest and juice, red onion, cilantro, and black pepper into a small mixing bowl; stir and allow to marinate until service.
2. Start by cleaning the Mexican shrimp. Reserve the shells for another recipe. Next, roast and peel the red bell peppers. Cut them into large pointed strips about 1 inch wide and reserve for service.
3. Steep the Earl Grey tea bags in hot water and allow the flavor to extract until time for deglazing.
4. Prepare asparagus, oranges, ginger root, and shallots.
5. Bring court bouillon to a boil. Add couscous and olive oil. Cover and remove from heat.
6. In 2 12-inch saute pans, heat olive oil. Saute the ginger, shallots, and shrimp together until seared for 2 minutes. Turn over the shrimp.
7. Add asparagus and roasted peppers. Deglaze with the Earl Grey tea, add orange zest and juice; reduce for 2 minutes.
8. Place a timbale mold of couscous at the top of the entree plate. Arrange a melange of shrimp, asparagus, and red pepper around the base of the plate and baste with tea sauce. Accompany with a tablespoon of papaya salsa next to the couscous. Serve immediately.
